entries.title: 4.4 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The fourth-generation S63B44T4 debuted in the F90 M5 before powering the M8 range. Competition models received 617hp, making this twin-turbo V8 one of BMW's most potent production engines .

commonFailures: Rod bearing wear - documented in high-performance applications, particularly with neglected oil changes
VANOS solenoid failure - can cause rough running and reduced power
Ignition coil failure - high-stress turbo operation accelerates wear; symptoms include misfires

entries.title: 4.4 Litre Petrol
entries.history: The facelift (LCI) version of the Competition engine (2022-2025) featuring refinements to cooling, turbo management, and durability. Addresses several early production issues while maintaining 617hp output .

commonFailures: Turbocharger failure - oil starvation or contamination can lead to premature turbo bearing seizure

Cooling system weaknesses - water pumps and radiators can fail under high thermal load

Spark plug misfire - high-stress operation accelerates plug degradation (30,000-40,000 mile interval)
HPFP failure - high-pressure fuel pump can fail, causing extended cranking and reduced power

commonProblems.h2: BMW M8 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The BMW M8’s S63 twin-turbo V8 delivers exceptional performance, but high thermal load and complex valvetrain components create predictable failure points as mileage climbs. The Competition variant (S63B44T4) and standard M8 (S63B44B) dominate replacement enquiries, with rod bearing wear, turbocharger failure, and timing chain guide degradation appearing most frequently between 50,000 and 90,000 miles. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below we break down the most common failures, real UK repair costs, and when replacement makes financial sense.

problems.h4: BMW M8 Rod Bearing Failure - Why S63 V8 Engines Spin Bearings

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump and oil pump, replace rod bearings with upgraded coated units, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught immediately after first knocking sound. Crank must be undamaged.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, crank regrind or replacement, new bearings, oil pump, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage bearing wear without catastrophic rod failure. Adds 60k+ miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt S63 engine with upgraded bearings, timing components, and bench testing
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution. Upgraded bearings prevent recurrence. 12-month warranty included.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a deep knocking sound from the bottom end that rises with RPM, stop driving immediately. Catching bearing wear before the crank is damaged makes the £3,800-£5,500 intermediate repair viable. However, most M8 owners discover the issue after significant damage has occurred. On a car worth £45,000-£70,000, the full replacement route (£14,995-£22,000) with upgraded bearings delivers better value than repeated major repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S63B44T4 and S63B44B engine prices for your BMW M8

problems.h4: BMW M8 Turbocharger Failure - Wastegate Rattle & Boost Loss

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator or bushings only, no turbo removal
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix. Only suitable for early wastegate rattle without oil loss or shaft play.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove affected turbo, replace with OEM-spec or upgraded unit, new oil feed lines
repairOptions.longevity: Best for single turbo failure. Address root cause with improved oil drainage.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Both Turbos + Engine Health Check
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace both turbos, oil lines, and perform compression test on both banks
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when one turbo fails at higher mileage. Prevents second failure within 12 months.

problems.recommendation: Replace both turbos simultaneously if your M8 has exceeded 60,000 miles. Labour to access the hot-vee is substantial - paying it twice within a year is false economy. Specialist pricing for a twin-turbo replacement (£5,500-£8,000) is approximately half the dealer cost and typically includes improved oil drain lines that reduce recurrence risk.
problems.cta: Get quotes for S63 turbo replacement for your BMW M8

problems.group: BMW M8 Timing Chain Guide Failure
problems.h4: BMW M8 Timing Chain Guide Failure - Cold Start Rattle

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain & Guide Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, guides, tensioners, and sprockets before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Ideal preventative work between 50,000-70,000 miles. Avoids engine destruction.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace damaged guides, chain, tensioners; inspect valves and pistons for contact
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if engine was not running when failure occurred and no valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement with rebuilt unit including upgraded timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Required if chain jumped teeth causing bent valves, damaged pistons, or cylinder wall scoring.

problems.recommendation: The S63 is an interference engine - timing failure almost always means engine destruction unless caught at the very first rattle. If your M8 makes a metallic rattling noise for 1-2 seconds on cold start, book preventative timing guide replacement immediately (£2,200-£3,200 specialist). This single repair can prevent a £15,000-£22,000 engine replacement. For LCI cars (2022+), failure rates are lower but not zero.
problems.cta: Compare S63 timing chain replacement costs for your BMW M8

problems.h4: BMW M8 High Oil Consumption - Valve Stem Seal & Piston Ring Wear

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Valve Stem Seal Replacement Only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace all 32 valve stem seals with upgraded Viton seals
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for consumption under 1L/1,000 miles without compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full top-end rebuild: new piston rings, valve stem seals, cylinder hone, head gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Required when compression is low or oil consumption exceeds 1.5L/1,000 miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine replacement with upgraded seals and rings designed for high thermal load
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when car has high mileage (80k+) and both bottom and top end show wear.

problems.recommendation: Monitor oil consumption carefully between services. If your M8 uses less than 1 litre per 3,000 miles, no action is needed. Once consumption exceeds 1 litre per 1,000 miles, valve stem seal replacement (£2,500-£3,800) is cost-effective. However, if compression is also down or the car has exceeded 80,000 miles, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded components (£14,995-£22,000) delivers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for S63 top-end rebuild for your BMW M8

problems.h4: BMW M8 Cooling System Failure - Overheating & Coolant Loss

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace failed component (expansion tank, hose, auxiliary pump or thermostat)
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for single component failure with no overheating damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace expansion tank, both auxiliary water pumps, all hoses, thermostat, coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended at 60k preventative maintenance or after any overheating event.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Engine with Overheating Damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine replacement required if overheating caused head gasket failure, warped heads, or bearing damage
repairOptions.longevity: Required when warning lights ignored and engine has overheated severely.

problems.recommendation: Never ignore a low coolant warning or rising temperature gauge on an M8. The S63 has minimal tolerance for overheating. For cars approaching 60,000 miles, a preventative cooling system overhaul (£1,500-£2,500 specialist) is some of the cheapest insurance you can buy. If you have already overheated, request a compression test and borescope inspection before committing to repairs.
problems.cta: Compare cooling system repair costs for your BMW M8

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your BMW M8
finalCta.paragraph: The M8’s S63 V8 is a precision engine that demands proactive maintenance. Rod bearing wear, turbo failure, timing chain guide degradation, high oil consumption, and cooling system weaknesses all follow predictable patterns - and all have cost-effective solutions when addressed early. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK BMW M8 specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components specifically designed to eliminate the original failure points. Every unit carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with nationwide delivery and supply & fit available across the UK. Compare prices, choose a specialist, and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare BMW M8 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific BMW M8 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: BMW M8 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the BMW M8 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes.

items.title: BMW M8 4.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The sole displacement powering every BMW M8 variant is the 4.4-litre S63 twin-turbo V8, shared with the F90 M5 and X5M/X6M. Introduced with the M8's June 2019 launch, this hot-vee configuration places the turbochargers inside the engine's V-angle for compact packaging and rapid response - though this design contributes to the thermal management challenges owners should monitor. Output varies by variant, with standard M8 models producing 600PS (591hp) and Competition versions reaching 625PS (617hp) at 6,000rpm.
commonFailurePoints: turbocharger failure on hot-vee units (45,000-75,000 miles)
valve stem seal hardening causing oil consumption
items.cta: Compare BMW M8 4.4L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: BMW M8 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the BMW M8 production timeline from 2019 to present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The M8 arrives in June 2019 with the S63B44T4 engine in Competition trim from launch - a first for BMW M, which traditionally launched standard models before higher-output versions.
keyChanges: June 2019: M8 Coupe (F92) and Convertible (F91) launched with Competition variant available from day one
October 2019: Gran Coupe (F93) four-door bodystyle added to lineup
December 2019: First M8 Competition models reach UK customers
Single engine across all variants: 4.4L S63 twin-turbo V8
knownFor: The most powerful BMW M car ever produced at launch with 617hp
Hot-vee turbo configuration creates exceptional response but high thermal load
First BMW M car to launch with Competition variant as a standard offering
Shared S63 engine with F90 M5 - proven but maintenance-intensive powertrain
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2020 BMW M8 replacement engines
years.ticker: BMW M8 Competition engine replacement • S63B44T4 engine for sale • BMW M8 rod bearing failure • M8 V8 engine cost UK

years.preview: Gran Coupe First Edition & Model Maturation
years.description: The four-door Gran Coupe proves popular with UK buyers, and limited-production First Edition models add exclusivity to the range.
keyChanges: First Edition models available across all three body styles
Gran Coupe now fully integrated into UK line-up
No mechanical changes to S63 engine family
Running production changes only - no major revisions
knownFor: Owners report first signs of cooling system weakness from 40,000 miles
Early cars begin showing wastegate rattle and turbo wear at higher mileages
Gran Coupe becomes the most practical M8 for daily driving
Enthusiasts note the First Edition's unique cosmetics but identical mechanicals
years.ticker: BMW M8 Gran Coupe engine replacement • S63 cooling system failure • M8 First Edition engine cost
enquiries: BMW M8 Gran Coupe engine replacement

years.description: The LCI (Life Cycle Impulse) facelift arrives for the 2022 model year with subtle styling updates and meaningful engine refinements - though the fundamental S63 architecture remains.
keyChanges: Visual facelift: revised lighting, grille, and interior trim
Updated engine management software for improved throttle response
Production continues unchanged until 2024 for most markets
mainEngines: S63B44B - 4.4L Petrol (standard model discontinued in some markets)
knownFor: LCI cars show reduced cooling system failure rates according to owner forums
Software updates address early throttle mapping complaints
UK market increasingly favours Competition spec over standard M8
Late LCI cars represent the most developed S63 iteration before discontinuation

faq.h2: BMW M8 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is the BMW M8 S63 engine reliable or should I be worried about big failures?
items.answer: The S63B44T4 in the M8 is BMW's most refined hot-vee V8, but high performance demands proactive maintenance. Later revisions are significantly improved over early S63 engines.
keyPoints: Rod bearing wear remains the most common catastrophic failure (55k-85k miles)
Turbocharger failures occur, particularly with neglected oil changes
LCI models (2022+) have improved cooling system durability
Regular 5k-6k mile oil changes dramatically extend engine life
The S63TU4 in the M8 is more reliable than earlier S63 iterations
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW M8 S63 engine replacement with upgraded bearings

items.question: What does a BMW M8 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt S63 engine for an M8 costs £14,995-£23,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,800-£3,500. Dealer prices run £22,000-£30,000 for a complete replacement.
keyPoints: Used engines: £8,500-£15,000 with 30k-60k miles
Reconditioned units: £14,000-£22,000 with factory tolerances
Competition models (S63B44T4) cost more than standard M8 units
Specialist supply & fit is approximately half the main dealer price
Core charges of £2,000-£3,000 apply on remanufactured engines
items.cta: Compare BMW M8 engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: How many miles will a BMW M8 engine last before needing replacement?
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance, an M8's S63 engine can reach 120k-150k miles. However, rod bearing and cooling system failures typically appear between 50k and 90k miles without proactive care.
keyPoints: Preventative bearing replacement at 60k miles extends life significantly
Cooling system overhaul at 60k miles prevents overheating damage
M8 Competition engines see higher thermal stress than standard units
Regular oil analysis can predict bearing wear before failure
Many M8 engines need major work by 80k-100k miles under normal ownership
items.cta: Find a low-mileage rebuilt BMW M8 engine with 12-month warranty

items.question: What are the most common BMW M8 engine problems I should look for when buying used?
items.answer: The four biggest red flags are cold start rattle (timing chain guides), deep knocking (rod bearings), blue startup smoke (valve seals), and coolant loss (cooling system leaks).
keyPoints: Listen for 1-2 second rattle on cold start - indicates timing guide wear
Any bottom-end knock means imminent rod bearing failure
Blue smoke on startup after sitting overnight signals valve seal wear
Check coolant level history - M8s consume coolant when stressed
Request service records showing oil changes every 5k-6k miles minimum
items.cta: Get a pre-purchase inspection quote for a used BMW M8

items.question: Should I replace or rebuild my BMW M8 engine?
items.answer: Replace with a rebuilt unit if your engine has suffered rod bearing or timing failure. If caught early with top-end issues only, a rebuild may be viable. Most owners choose replacement for the upgraded components.
keyPoints: Full replacement with upgraded bearings costs £14,995-£22,000 supply only
Top-end rebuild (valve seals, timing guides) costs £5,500-£8,500
Bottom-end failure almost always requires full replacement
Rebuilt engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Upgrade to coated bearings prevents the original failure point
items.cta: Compare BMW M8 engine rebuild vs replacement costs

items.question: Does the BMW M8 Competition have the same engine as the standard M8?
items.answer: Both use the 4.4L S63 twin-turbo V8, but the Competition gets the S63B44T4 with 617hp versus 600hp in the standard M8's S63B44B. The blocks are similar but tuning and some internals differ.
keyPoints: Competition produces 617hp (625PS), standard M8 produces 600hp
The T4 in S63B44T4 denotes the fourth-generation Competition tune
Competition models dominate replacement enquiries due to higher sales volumes
Both share the same rod bearing and cooling system weaknesses
Engine swap between variants requires ECU reprogramming
items.cta: Compare BMW M8 Competition engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Does the BMW M8 have an oil consumption problem?
items.answer: Some oil consumption is normal on the S63, especially with sporty driving. Excessive consumption (over 1L per 1,000 miles) indicates worn valve stem seals, piston rings, or turbo seals.
keyPoints: BMW considers up to 1L per 1,000 miles within specification
Valve stem seals harden under extreme heat - common from 60k miles
Blue smoke on startup is the classic symptom of seal failure
Regular oil level checks between services are essential
Upgraded Viton seals in rebuilt engines solve the problem permanently
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW M8 valve stem seal replacement

items.question: Which BMW M8 year is most reliable and which should I avoid?
items.answer: 2022+ LCI models are the most reliable M8s, benefiting from cooling system and software refinements. 2019-2020 early cars have the highest documented failure rates, particularly for cooling and turbo components.
keyPoints: LCI (2022-2025) has improved coolant hose and expansion tank design
Pre-LCI (2019-2021) cars show more cooling system and wastegate issues
Final Edition (2024-2025) benefits from all cumulative improvements
Any M8 with full service history is preferable to a neglected newer car
Low mileage doesn't guarantee health - heat cycles matter more than miles
items.cta: Compare BMW M8 engine prices by year and variant

items.question: Is the BMW M8 Gran Coupe engine different from the Coupe?
items.answer: The engine and S63B44T4/S63B44B codes are identical across all M8 body styles (Coupe F92, Convertible F91, Gran Coupe F93). Performance figures are the same regardless of bodyshell.
keyPoints: All variants use the same 4.4L twin-turbo S63 V8 engine family
No power difference between Coupe, Convertible, or Gran Coupe
Gran Coupe carries slightly more weight but shares powertrain completely
Replacement costs are identical across all three body styles
The Gran Coupe is the most common M8 on UK roads
items.cta: Get quotes for BMW M8 Gran Coupe engine replacement

items.question: Can I tune or remap my BMW M8 engine safely?
items.answer: The S63 has significant tuning potential, but any power increase accelerates wear on rod bearings, turbos, and cooling systems. Stick to stock unless you accept dramatically shorter engine life.
keyPoints: Stock Competition already produces 617hp - tuning adds little real-world benefit
Remapped cars show much higher rod bearing failure rates
Most specialist rebuilders refuse warranty on tuned engines
Cooling system upgrades are essential for any power increase
A stock M8 with proper maintenance outlasts any tuned car
items.cta: Compare standard vs rebuilt BMW M8 engine prices - no tuning needed
